"Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people." The very brief verse notes the penalty for imitating the holy incense. The grammatical voices note the penalty. Keil and Delitzsch and Gill note that "whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people" — whoever made a like incense (imitated the holy composition) for his own use (to smell, to enjoy the fragrance) was to be cut off from the people; the penalty (excision, the cutting-off, for profaning the holy by imitation); the holiness guarded (the sacred incense not to be counterfeited or put to common use, on pain of cutting-off).

On "whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto," the witnesses note the profaning. Gill notes the imitating of the incense (for common enjoyment) — the profanation; the sin against the holy.
